data.title: United Airlines outlines $9–$11 full-year EPS target as macro environment stabilizes and premium strategy intensifies
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: United Airlines Holdings (UAL) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO J. Scott Kirby stated the quarter was further evidence that the "United -- next strategy continues to work and that the 2 brand loyal revenue diverse airlines continue to generate the bulk of industry profit." He highlighted operational execution in a volatile macro environment, particularly noting challenges at Newark, and pointed to a recent inflection point in demand: "As uncertainty has declined, we've seen an improvement in booked revenue, including a double-digit acceleration in business demand."
  • Kirby emphasized the company's structural advantages, noting, "The 2 brand loyal airlines continue to just gradually win share quarter-over-quarter and the advantages that we have are structural, permanent, irreversible and they're growing and it's simply not practical to copy them."
  • COO Torbjorn J. Enqvist addressed operational performance, with a focus on Newark, citing, "Cementing the progress we have made over the last couple of months to improve the resilience of our Newark operation is a huge priority for United." He reported that bookings at Newark have largely recovered, and the airport is now operating "within its capability."
  • CCO Andrew P. Nocella reported, "United's top line revenue increased 1.7% to a record $15.2 billion in the quarter." He highlighted premium cabin revenue growth of 5.6% year-over-year and noted, "Premium RASMs were 6 points better than non-premium."
  • CFO Michael D. Leskinen stated, "We delivered earnings per share of $3.87, well within our guidance range and ahead of Wall Street expectations of $3.81." He emphasized the company's cost discipline, noting, "We continue to run better than planned, and I'm pleased with the 2.2% CASM-ex growth we delivered in the second quarter."

Outlook

  • Leskinen indicated, "We expect third quarter EPS to be between $2.25 and $2.75."
  • Full-year EPS guidance was reiterated: "We expect full year EPS to be between $9 and $11."
  • Nocella described the demand environment as having experienced "a 6-point positive swing in sales to date in July versus the second quarter, but even more importantly, a double-digit swing in higher-yielding business revenues."
  • Management expects the impact from Newark disruptions to linger in Q3, with about 1 point margin impact, but no expected effect in Q4.
  • Nocella noted upcoming international expansion, including service to Thailand, Vietnam, and the Philippines, and a planned return to JFK in 2027.

Financial Results

  • United reported $15.2 billion in revenue for Q2 2025.
  • Earnings per share were $3.87 for the quarter.
  • Free cash flow generation exceeded $1.1 billion in Q2.
  • Liquidity at quarter-end was $18.6 billion, including a $3 billion undrawn revolver.
  • The company paid down $1.5 billion of MileagePlus bonds two years early, fully unencumbering the MileagePlus business.
  • United repurchased $235 million worth of shares at an average price of $66 during the quarter, with $829 million remaining in authorization.
  • Leskinen noted, "We now expect to generate over $2 billion in free cash flow for the year."

Q&A

  • Thomas John Fitzgerald, TD Cowen: Asked for an update on Connected Media, to which Nocella responded United seeks to "double our revenues in 2025 versus 2024 in the media revenue channel" and highlighted the integration of seatback screens and Starlink for future value.
  • Catherine Maureen O'Brien, Goldman Sachs: Queried about cost tailwinds to offset the flight attendant contract; Leskinen replied, "We've made some big changes in our procurement department, and we're seeing some real savings out of the supply chain. We're also doing a better job of managing inventory of parts."
  • Leslie Josephs, CNBC: Asked about MAX 10 deliveries and premium capacity; Leskinen said, "We're still hopeful that we can have some deliveries in 2027. But we right now have a contingency plan to be able to take MAX 9s and continue our upgauging strategy with MAX 9s."
  • Mary Schlangenstein, Bloomberg: Inquired about Starlink issues; Enqvist confirmed, "It's pretty much been resolved... we've got 60 airplanes flying around right now, and we think that the issue is behind us."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed curiosity and cautious optimism, often probing for sustainability of cost controls, the recovery in demand, and the impact of operational challenges. The tone was neutral to slightly positive, with focused questions on execution and margin levers.
  • Management maintained a confident and positive tone in both prepared remarks and responses, with repeated emphasis on structural advantages and resilience. Leskinen stated, "I remain excited about the future of United Airlines, and we will continue to work to deliver on our financial commitments."
  • Compared to Q1, both analysts and management displayed improved confidence. Management's language shifted from cautious optimism to more assertive statements on demand recovery and cost performance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for full-year EPS remains, but the range has narrowed to $9–$11, reflecting improved clarity versus the wider range and contingency language in Q1.
  • Management commentary now highlights a "meaningful inflection point in demand" and a "6-point positive swing" in recent sales, whereas last quarter's focus was on demand stabilization and cautious capacity reductions.
  • Analysts in Q2 are more focused on specific operational and product questions, whereas in Q1, conversations centered more on macroeconomic risk and downside recession scenarios.
  • Premium strategy is further emphasized in Q2, with new product launches and premium cabin expansion discussed at length.
  • Financially, Q2 features stronger revenue, higher EPS, and significant debt paydown compared to Q1's more defensive posture.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing risks from macroeconomic uncertainty and potential lingering effects from Newark disruptions into Q3.
  • There are continued industry risks associated with supply chain constraints, particularly for wide-body aircraft and engines.
  • Operational risks related to weather and FAA technology outages remain, but management outlined mitigation steps such as capacity controls and infrastructure upgrades at Newark.
  • Analysts raised concerns about sustainability of cost controls, supply chain delays, and timing of new aircraft deliveries.

Final Takeaway

United Airlines management emphasized the company's resilience and strategic focus on premium products, operational reliability, and revenue diversity. With demand showing recent positive momentum, cost control efforts delivering results, and a reinforced premium strategy, United reaffirmed its full-year EPS guidance while signaling confidence in continued margin expansion and free cash flow generation as the macro environment stabilizes.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Talen Energy to buy Ohio, Pennsylvania power plants for $3.5B
data.description:
Talen Energy (NASDAQ:TLN) +14.8% post-market Thursday after saying it agreed to acquire a power plant in Pennsylvania and another in Ohio for ~$3.5B after adjusting for estimated tax benefits.

Talen (NASDAQ:TLN) said it will purchase Caithness Energy's Moxie Freedom Energy Center in Pennsylvania and Caithness Energy and BlackRock's (BLK) Guernsey Power Station in Ohio, both combined-cycle gas-fired plants located in the PJM power market.

The company said the net purchase price reflects an acquisition multiple of 6.7x 2026 EV/EBITDA for "two of the most efficient natural gas plants in PJM," and it expects the deals will be immediately accretive to free cash flow per share by more than 40% in 2026 and more than 50% through 2029.

Talen (NASDAQ:TLN) said it will issue ~$3.8B in new debt to fund the acquisitions and refinance target debt, using both secured and unsecured instruments.

"The addition of the facilities to Talen's portfolio enhances Talen's ability to offer reliable, scalable, grid-supported and regionally diverse low-carbon capacity to hyperscale data centers and large commercial offtakers," the company said.

data.title: Granite joint venture awarded $158M Guam Defense System project
data.description:
• Granite (NYSE:GVA) said on Thursday that its joint venture with Obayashi Corporation has received a federally funded $158M task order from the Naval Facilities Engineering Command (NAVFAC).
  • The project, issued under the Facilities and Infrastructure Multiple Award Construction Contract (F&I MACC), involves the construction of the Missile Defense Agency (MDA) P694-2 PDI: Guam Defense System, Enhanced Integrated Air and Missile Defense (EIAMD), Phase 1, in South Finegayan. The award will be reflected in Granite’s third-quarter CAP.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bank OZK Q2 earnings beat, helped by growth in loans, deposits
data.description:
Bank OZK (NASDAQ:OZK) stock gained 1.1% in Thursday after-hours trading after the bank posted record earnings that exceeded the Wall Street consensus.

Q2 GAAP EPS of $1.58, topping the consensus estimate of $1.55, rose from $1.47 in Q1 and $1.52 in last year's Q2.

"Our excellent results for the quarter included record net income, record EPS, record net interest income, excellent growth in loans and deposits, and solid asset quality," said Chairman and CEO George Gleason.

"Our strong results for the first half of the year put us in a great position to achieve" its goal of improving on its record 2024 net income and EPS, management said. 

Provision for credit losses dropped to $35.2M from $38.4M in the prior quarter and $73.6M a year ago. 

Net interest income of $397M, vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of $388M, increased from $375M in Q1 and $388M in last year's Q2. Net interest margin of 4.36% improved from 4.31% in Q1 and fell from 4.68% in Q2 2024.

"We continue to expect to achieve record net interest income for the full year of 2025," the company said.

Noninterest income of $31.3M compared with $34.7M in the previous quarter and $28.8M a year ago.

Pretax preprovision net revenue of $274.9M increased from $262.3M in Q1 and fell from $279.3M in last year's Q2.

Noninterest expense of $153.2M increased from $147.0M in Q1 and $137.5M in Q2 2024.

Bank OZK's (NASDAQ:OZK) Real Estate Specialties Group ("RESG") loan originations totaled $1.51B, up from $1.21B in the previous quarter and down from $1.60B in the year-ago quarter. "Many sponsors remain appropriately cautious regarding new projects given the heightened level of macroeconomic uncertainty and the continued tight Fed monetary policy," the company said.

Loans grew to $33.0B at June 30, 2025, from $31.1B at March 31, 2025. Deposits increased to $33.5B from $31.9B.

Conference call on July 18 at 8:30 AM ET.

Earlier, Bank OZK GAAP EPS of $1.58 beats by $0.03, revenue of $428M beats by $4.75M

data.title: Interactive Brokers stock perks up after Q2 earnings beat on higher trading volumes
data.description:
Interactive Brokers (NASDAQ:IBKR) stock climbed 4.8% in Thursday after-hours trading after posting better-than-expected second-quarter headline results, bolstered by higher customer trading volume.

Q2 2025 adjusted EPS of $0.51, topping the $0.47 average analyst estimate, dropped from $1.88 in the prior quarter, but advanced from $0.41 a year ago.

Total net revenue of $1.48B in Q2, surpassing the $1.40B consensus, rose from $1.40B in Q1 and $1.23B in Q2 2024.

Total daily average revenue trades accelerated to 3.55M from 3.52M in Q1 and +49% from the year-earlier period.

Commission revenue was $516M, up from $514M in the previous quarter and +27% Y/Y. Customer trading volume in stocks, options and futures increased 31%, 24% and 18%, respectively, Y/Y.

Execution, clearing, and distribution fees of $116M in Q2 compared with $121M in Q1 and inched up 1% from last year's Q2.

Net interest income accelerated to $860M from $770M in Q1 and $792M a year before.

The number of customer accounts on Interactive Brokers (NASDAQ:IBKR) of 3.87M rose from 3.62M in Q1 and +32% from Q4 2024.

data.title: F.N.B. GAAP EPS of $0.36 beats by $0.02, revenue of $438.2M beats by $14.03M
data.description:
• F.N.B. press release (NYSE:FNB): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.36 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $438.2M (+8.5% Y/Y) beats by $14.03M.

data.title: Orion Properties gets a revised acquisition offer from Kawa Capital
data.description:
Orion Properties (NYSE:ONL) received a revised unsolicited, non-binding offer from Kawa Capital for the proposed acquisition of all the outstanding shares the latter does not already own in the office REIT.

Shares were +3.90% post market to $2.66.

Kawa Capital has offered to acquire the shares of common stock for a cash consideration of $2.75 per share.

The board of Orion Properties had unanimously rejected Kawa Capital's $2.50 per share offer on July 9.

The company said its board will carefully review and evaluate the revised indication of interest, and does not intend to comment further on the offer until the board has completed its review.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Netflix posts earnings beat, raises FY25 revenue guidance
Key takeaways from Netflix's Q2, with stock dipping
data.description:
Netflix stock (NASDAQ:NFLX) slipped 1% in immediate postmarket trading after the company's second-quarter earnings beat expectations on top and bottom lines and the firm raised full-year revenue expectations.

Revenues grew nearly 16% year-over-year to land at $11.08B, topping $11.06B expected. Operating income jumped to $3.78B from a year-ago $2.6B, and up from last quarter's $3.35B. Meanwhile, operating margin ticked up to 34.1% from a year-ago 27.2% and last quarter's 31.7%.

Net income jumped 46% year-over-year, to $3.13B.

With Netflix having stopped reporting subscriber growth, investor attention has naturally shifted to key financials, notably including cash flows. And a pivot to advertising-supported subscriptions and an ongoing move into live programming offers investors more color to seek out around earnings reports.

For the full year, it's raised revenue expectations to $44.8B-$45.2B (from $43.5B-$44.5B) and now expects currency-neutral operating margin of 29.5%, up from 29% (30% on an as-reported basis).

Cash flow

Net cash from operations rose year-over-year, to $2.42B, but dipped from Q1's $2.79B. Similarly, free cash flow rose to $2.27B from a year-ago $1.21B, but was down from last quarter's $2.66B.

"Our capital allocation approach is unchanged—we prioritize profitable growth by reinvesting in our business, maintaining ample liquidity and returning excess cash (beyond several billion dollars of minimum cash and any used for selective M&A) to shareholders through share repurchases," the company noted. 

It boosted full-year free cash flow expectations to $8B-$8.5B thanks to a corresponding increase in its forecasts for revenue and operating margin.

Gross debt at quarter-end was $14.5B, and cash and equivalents came to $8.2B. The company noted starting a commercial paper program in May, giving it the ability to issue short-term unsecured notes of up to $3B.

Viewing metrics

The company's content slate has been notably loaded toward the second half of 2025, but even so, viewers watched 95B hours in the first half (up 1% year-over-year).

"Our viewing is very broad and we’re not dependent on any one title to drive engagement—forinstance, even our biggest titles that have tens of millions of views account for less than 1% oftotal viewing on Netflix in the reporting period," Netflix said.

And live programming is expanding, including two marquee boxing matches in the third quarter and another NFL doubleheader on Christmas Day.

Monetization

"Response to our recent price adjustments, as measured by member acquisition, churn and plan mix, has been broadly in line with our expectations," the company said. 

Meanwhile, on ad-supported progress: "We continue to make progress building our ads business and still expect to roughly double ads revenue in 2025." Netflix's U.S. upfronts are nearly complete with the "vast majority" of deals with major agencies already closed.

Netflix has focused on building out more capabilities for advertisers, including the rollout of its own first-party ad-tech platform, the Netflix Ads Suite.

The company's usual live earnings executive interview will go live at 4:45 p.m. ET.

data.title: RE/MAX announces higher June home sales report
data.description:
• RE/MAX June 2025 marks 5 straight months of home sales gains across 50 metro areas, with inventory up 30% YoY and median prices hitting $440,000.

  • Sales activity in June edged up 1.3% from May 2025 and rose 5.7% compared to June of last year.

  • Source: Press release
